retry_reason: Allocation failure is the only failure mode; refcount_set always succeeds. Retrying the same allocation under unchanged memory pressure is unlikely to succeed, and a retry would require caller-level judgement.
contract_summary: Constructor/set-wrapper: allocates a new extent_state object from the slab cache, initializes its fields (including state=0, RB node, leak tracking, waitqueue, and trace), and sets the refcount to 1 via refcount_set. The caller receives a fully initialized object with one reference. The caller is responsible for releasing this reference (e.g., via free_extent_state or similar put operation) when the object is no longer needed. If allocation fails, NULL is returned and the caller must handle the error accordingly.
